title: No per-item schema-validation diagnostic — which item, field, and constraint fail a schema is reported nowhere
description: No surface reports which item id, field, and constraint cause a block's items to fail a schema. update's resyncSchema discards the AJV error at an unbound catch (index.ts:622), returning a bare {status:blocked}. context-validate runs referential-integrity + invariant checks only, not per-item schema validation. No op validates a block's items against a target schema and returns the failures. The AJV detail is fully present at the throw site (allErrors:true; instancePath, keyword, params, message) and dropped. An operator facing a blocked schema must hand-validate the block against the catalog schema to learn what failed.

evidence.reference: resyncSchema's catch is unbound — the thrown ValidationError carrying errors[] is discarded; returns {status:blocked, registeredMigrations:[]}
validate compiles with allErrors:true and throws ValidationError holding the full per-item AJV errors[] (instancePath/keyword/params/message)
validateContext checks edge referential integrity + config-declared invariants only; it never AJV-validates block items against their schemas

impact: A blocked schema is opaque: the operator cannot see which item or field failed, cannot distinguish a real invalid item from a migrate/validate fault, and must reverse-engineer the failure by hand-validating the block.
proposed_resolution: A returns-not-throws per-item validator — block items against a target schema version yielding per-item {item id, field, keyword, params} — surfaced both as a standalone op and within update's blocked output, mapping instancePath /<arrayKey>/N to the item id. Reuses the AJV errors validate/validateBlockWithMigrationForDir already produce; shares the in-memory migrate+validate primitive with FGAP-066/TASK-046. Distinct from FGAP-076 (partial-application legibility).
